skill.name: website-to-video
skill.description: Capture a general website or URL and turn it into a HyperFrames video using screenshots, brand assets, storyboard steps, narration, and validation checks.
skill.summary: Create a site tour or social showcase video from a captured website.
skill.author: heygen-com

security_audit.summary: AI review found no confirmed malicious intent and no prompt injection attempt. Most static findings are false positives from markdown examples, placeholders, and validation text, but the skill legitimately captures websites, stores screenshots and assets, uses external APIs, and handles API keys, so it should publish with clear data handling warnings.

medium_findings.title: Arbitrary Website Capture And Local Artifact Storage
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The workflow asks the agent to capture a user supplied URL and store screenshots, assets, extracted text, and metadata locally. This can collect sensitive website content if the user captures private or authenticated pages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The capture command and artifact list are explicit. The use is core to the skill, but sensitive pages could expose data in local project files.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party AI Processing Of Captured Screenshots
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The workflow recommends Gemini image descriptions and snapshot descriptions, which can send captured page images or frames to a third-party AI service. Users should avoid private pages unless they approve that processing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files explicitly describe Gemini powered image descriptions and snapshot descriptions. This is intended behavior, but it is a real data sharing consideration.

medium_findings.title: API Key Handling Guidance Needs Care
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE with legitimate purpose. The workflow discusses Gemini, ElevenLabs, HeyGen, and OAuth tokens, and it tells agents to use keys from environment variables or pasted user input. This is not exfiltration, but it increases secret handling risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain direct API key setup and API call guidance. The endpoints are legitimate service APIs, but pasted secrets and .env files require caution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Caused Many False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most external command hits. The flagged text is mostly markdown instructions and example commands such as HyperFrames capture, preview, lint, and validation. These are expected for a video rendering workflow, although users should run them only in trusted projects.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible workflow steps rather than hidden execution. They are not automatically run by the skill file itself.

low_findings.title: Verification Script Static Exec Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The verification script states it performs pure file analysis and contains no shell spawns in the reviewed sections. The scanner appears to have matched process control text and JavaScript tokens rather than executable command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script imports filesystem helpers and exits with status codes, but the reviewed code path does not spawn a shell or evaluate user supplied code.

low_findings.title: Bundled Sound Effect Metadata Is Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Weak crypto and command alerts in the bundled SFX files are metadata and markdown references to MP3 names, descriptions, and license credits. No cryptographic or executable behavior was found there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected files are static JSON metadata and credits. The flagged tokens are filenames, words, and markdown formatting.

low_findings.title: Path Placeholder Alerts Are Mostly Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE with minor caution. Many path traversal and filesystem alerts come from documented placeholders such as project directories, capture folders, and snapshot output paths. They describe normal local project files, not an instruction to read protected system paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths are scoped to project output and validation artifacts. The skill still creates and reads many local files, so filesystem access remains a real operational factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Sensitive Data May Enter Capture Outputs
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow can write screenshots, visible text, extracted assets, and generated analysis into the project directory. This is expected, but private websites or authenticated sessions can place sensitive data in local artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The output folders and extracted artifact types are explicitly listed. The risk depends on the sensitivity of the target website.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secrets Are Used By External Service Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes direct API examples for ElevenLabs, HeyGen, and Gemini-related snapshot workflows. These examples are legitimate, but marketplace users should understand that keys authorize third-party service calls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The examples clearly use environment variables in request headers and reference Gemini keys. This is not hidden, but it is sensitive operational behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams often need a polished video tour, but turning a live website into a motion piece takes many manual steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through capture, storyboard, build, narration, and validation.
actual_capabilities: Captures a public website into screenshots, assets, fonts, text, and design data.
Guides brand analysis, storyboard planning, script writing, and scene timing.
Builds HyperFrames HTML compositions for site tours and social clips.
Supports narration workflows with HeyGen, ElevenLabs, or local Kokoro speech.
Adds validation gates for linting, snapshots, beat review, audio timing, and preview delivery.
Provides a verification script for artifact checks, motion coverage, SFX timing, and duration consistency.
limitations: It is for general website tours, not product launch promos or topic explainers.
Private or authenticated websites may expose sensitive screenshots and text in local outputs.
External voice and image description services may require API keys and may process captured content.
Final MP4 rendering is on demand; the default deliverable is a Studio preview URL.

use_cases.title: Portfolio Site Tour
use_cases.description: Create a short video that highlights a portfolio website using its existing visuals, typography, and page structure.

use_cases.title: Campaign Landing Page Clip
use_cases.description: Turn a campaign page into a social video that shows the main message, proof points, and call to action.

use_cases.title: Website Redesign Showcase
use_cases.description: Build a guided walkthrough that presents a redesigned homepage or content site to stakeholders.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Site Tour
prompt_templates.prompt: Use website-to-video to turn https://example.com into a 20 second landscape site tour. Ask me only for missing essentials.

prompt_templates.title: Social Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: A vertical video for social channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15 second portrait social clip from this website. Use the site visuals, keep narration optional, and include a clear final CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Narrated Walkthrough
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 45 second narrated website walkthrough. Capture the site, write a storyboard, generate timing, and validate the final HyperFrames preview.

prompt_templates.title: Autonomous Showcase
prompt_templates.prompt: Use autonomous mode for a polished website showcase. Decide pacing, visual treatment, music use, and captions, but keep all validation gates.

output_examples.input: A user provides a public agency homepage and asks for a short landscape showcase.
output: A captured asset set with screenshots, brand colors, fonts, and visible text.
A storyboard, narration script, timed beats, and HyperFrames composition files.
A Studio preview URL with validation notes and items the user should spot-check.

output_examples.input: A team asks for a vertical clip from a campaign landing page with no narration.
output: A concise motion plan using the page hero, product screenshots, and branded CTA.
A portrait HyperFrames preview with beat timing, SFX placement, and snapshot review.
A delivery summary that separates verified checks from unverified audio or motion items.

best_practices: Use public or approved pages, and avoid private dashboards unless the user accepts local capture artifacts.
Decide the core message and audience before choosing visuals or narration.
Run the required HyperFrames lint, validate, snapshot, and verification checks before delivery.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for a product launch promo when the product-launch workflow fits better.
Do not paste API keys into shared logs or commit generated .env files.
Do not skip validation gates just because the workflow is in autonomous mode.

faq.question: What kind of websites does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports general public websites, landing pages, portfolios, blogs, and content sites that can be captured in a browser.

faq.question: Does it create a product launch video?
faq.answer: No. For product launch or promo work, use the product launch workflow instead of this website tour workflow.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: Base capture does not require keys. Gemini, HeyGen, or ElevenLabs features require the relevant service credentials.

faq.question: Where does captured website data go?
faq.answer: The workflow stores screenshots, assets, extracted text, and build files in the local project directory.

faq.question: Can it render the final MP4?
faq.answer: Yes, but rendering is on demand. The normal handoff is a HyperFrames Studio preview for review.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The marketplace report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
